National Science Day

# National Science Day

[](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

![Ratnakar Sadasyula's avatar](https://substackcdn.com/image/fetch/$s_!R73I!,w_36,h_36,c_fill,f_auto,q_auto:good,fl_progressive:steep/https%3A%2F%2Fsubstack-post-media.s3.amazonaws.com%2Fpublic%2Fimages%2F81a2c542-8b3f-4ade-98d2-a40950cffbe3_2640x1980.jpeg)

[Ratnakar Sadasyula](https://substack.com/@sadashree)

Feb 28, 2021

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

Share

National Science Day is to commemorate the discovery of Raman Effect by Shri C.V.Raman. On this occasion presenting a compilation of my posts on Indian scientists and science here.

*“The impetus given to astrophysics by Saha’s work can scarcely be overestimated, as nearly all later progress in this field has been influenced by it and much of the subsequent work has the character of refinements of Saha’s ideas.”- S.Rosseland*

[Meghnad Saha](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2020/02/14/meghnad-saha/)

*In those days when success was measured, by a steady job, and a comfortable life, it was the passion for physics that was pulling the young man. His parents gave in to his wishes and were sending him to Cambridge. The young lad, would soon become one of the greatest scientists of the 20th century, the father of India’s nuclear program,**Homi Jahangir Bhaba.***

[Homi Jahangir Bhaba.](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2019/11/01/homi-jahangir-bhaba/)

*One of the few Indian organizations, we can be justifiably proud of is ISRO. Be it the first satellite Arybhatta to Mars Mission to recent Chandrayaan, which was an audacious attempt. Yet not much is known about the man, who actually shaped ISRO to what it is today. While[Vikram Sarabhai](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/08/12/vikram-sarabhai/), is well known as the founder of ISRO, the man who shaped it’s organizational structure and put in a fine process in place, was it’s 3rd chairman- Satish Dhawan, after whom the Sriharikota Space Center is named.*

[Satish Dhawan](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2019/09/24/satish-dhawan/)

*“C.V. Raman was the first to recognize and demonstrate that the energy of photon can undergo partial transformation within matter. I still recall vividly the deep impression that this discovery made on all of us….”- Albert Einstein*

[C.V. Raman](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/11/21/c-v-raman/)

*On Dec 30, 1971 Vikram Sarabhai passed away at Kovalam,of a sudden heart attack, on one of his visits to Thumba. One of the greatest scientists of modern India was no more, and yet the legacy he left behind- ISRO, IIM, and the chain of institutions all over India, would ensure he would never be forgotten.*

[Vikram Sarabhai](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/08/12/vikram-sarabhai/)

*Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar was a bridge between the two worlds, one of the greatest Indian scientists of the modern era, who was an equally good writer. To most Indians, his name would be familiar with CSIR, that he founded as well as the awards given out in his name to aspiring scientists. Yet not many know that he was also an equally good Urdu poet under the name of Seemab, and composed the Kulgeet for Benaras Hindu University in Sanskrit.*

[Shanti Swarup Bhatnagar](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/02/21/shanti-swaroop-bhatnagar/)

*One such scientist was Dr. Hargobind Khorana who won the Nobel in 1968 along with Marshall Nirenberg and Robert Holley, for showing how order of nucleotides in nucleic acids, that carry the cell’s genetic code control the synthesis of proteins.*

[Dr. Hargobind Khorana](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/01/09/dr-hargobind-khorana/)

*Jayant Narlikar rated S.N.Bose’s work on particle statistics as one of the Top 10 Achievements of Indian science in 20th century. When asked about the Nobel, S.N.Bose simply remarked – I got all the recognition that I deserve, he was not too hung up on it.The fact that his research would play an important role in scientifc discourse was more important than the Nobel.*

[S.N.Bose](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/01/01/s-n-bose-the-god-particle/)

*To call J.C.Bose just a scientist, would however be akin to calling, Leonardo Da Vinci a mere painter, this man was a true polymath, whose genius transcended boundaries. What do you say of a man who was a physicist, biologist, and has been named by IEEE as one of the fathers of radio science. A man who was not just a scientist, who discovered that plants too could have feelings, but also wrote science fiction in Bengali. He was also an inventor, who apart from the crescograph invented many other instruments for research in physics and made significant contribution to the field of chronobiology.*

[J.C.Bose](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2015/11/30/acharya-jagdish-chandra-bose/)

.But yet if one were to compile a history of medicine, in the world, the name of Dr.Subbarow, would be standing right up there along with other greats like Alexander Fleming, Ronald Ross. Subbarow made some of the most significant discoveries in the field of medicine, yet forget about ordinary people, even in the scientific community, his name is not too well known.

[Dr.Subbarow](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2015/01/12/dr-yellapragada-subbarow/)

*The bonding between Hardy and Ramanujan was much earlier though. Exactly a year back, Hardy received a letter from Ramanujan, then working as a clerk at Madras Port Trust. It was a 10 page letter, which had around 120 statements on theorems, continued fractions and number theory. Initially Hardy dismissed that letter as a crank, the ones mathematicians usually get, but a second look made him revise his opinion. He discussed it over with his colleague, J.E.Littlewood, and their conclusions“the results must be true because, if they were not true, no one would have had the imagination to invent them”.*

[Ramanujan](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2013/12/22/the-man-who-knew-infinity/)

*Dr.Raja Ramanna, one of the great Indian scientists, man behind Pokhran I, played a critical role in our nuclear program during the early stages, and associated with it for around 40 years, a skilled pianist too.*

[Dr.Raja Ramanna](https://historyunderyourfeet.substack.com/p/dr-raja-ramanna)

*Not many know about G.N. Ramachandran or GNR as he was known, one of the great Indian scientists of modern era, known for his discovery of the collagen structure.And apart from being a scientist of note, was an equally good Vedic scholar, studied the Upanishads in depth and interpreted their teachings. His contributions in molecular biophysics, especially protein structure, were worthy of a Noble, which like most other Indian scientists was denied.*

[G.N. Ramachandran](https://historyunderyourfeet.substack.com/p/gnramachandran)

*When the annals of modern Indian science are written, the name of Prafulla Chandra Ray will be one of those in golden letters. The father of modern Indian Chemistry, the first Indian to set up a pharma company, and a pioneer of modern chemical industries in India.*

[P.C.Ray](https://historyunderyourfeet.wordpress.com/2018/08/02/p-c-ray/)

This is my humble tribute to these great Indian scientists who produced some outstanding work against all odds like rudimentary equipment, discrimination during the British era, lack of funds. People like Dr. Yellapragada Subbarow, Acharya J.C.Bose, S.N.Bose, Meghnad Saha, G.N. Ramachandran have produced some outstanding work, on par with any of the Nobel Laureates. Yet none have got the recognition due.

Sadly we have failed to highlight the achievements of our Indian scientists, and these are genuine achievements. It is due to this we have people coming up with outlandish claims of achievements we never had.

What really has stopped the academia, ruling elite from highlighting the achievements of our modern scientists?

IEEE has recognized J.C.Bose as the one of the pioneers of radio science, but our textbooks still keep teaching that Marconi invented radio.

Except in medical field, no one is even aware of the sterling contributions of Dr.Yellapragada Subbarao, when his contribution has been no less than any one.

See the irony, scientists who did research on Bosons, Bose-Einstein statistics were given Nobel, but S.N.Bose himself did not get it. And none from this country even raised this issue.

Our scientists did some of the best ever research work ever, built up some top class institutions, but they never got the credit due. And then we will whine about how we have no role models. Mera Bharat Mahaan, truly.
